# Ghost Layer — Ephemeral Execution Layer for Autonomous Agents
> Give your AI agent a body that vanishes. Spawn → execute → evaporate → leave signed residue.

## What Ghost Layer Is
Ghost Layer is a Python CLI tool and reverse-proxy library for running AI agents with scoped, time-limited credentials. It is NOT an MCP server.

The core loop:
1. `ghost spawn` — Creates an ephemeral session with a fresh Ed25519 signing key, a bearer token, a TTL (default 300s), and optional tool scopes
2. `ghost possess` — Binds an agent to the session via a local intercept proxy
3. `ghost act` — Records a signed, scoped action against the session (enforces allowed scopes)
4. `ghost evaporate` — Destroys the signing key, finalizes the signed action chain (residue)
5. `ghost replay` — Retrieves and cryptographically verifies the signed execution history

## GhostGateway (Reverse Proxy / Egress Broker)
`ghost serve` starts a reverse proxy gateway that agents route through.

Sidecar mode (your own service):
  ghost serve --upstream http://localhost:8080

Broker mode (third-party API — real key never reaches the agent):
  ghost serve --upstream https://api.openai.com --upstream-key $UPSTREAM_KEY_FROM_ENV

When `ghost evaporate` is called, the gateway IMMEDIATELY rejects any further requests with that token — even if the agent cached it.

Default gateway port: 7391

## Install
  pip install ghost-layer

## CLI Reference
  ghost spawn   --intent "deploy_staging" --ttl 300 --scope aws_ec2 --scope s3
  ghost possess --agent openai://gpt-4 --session-id <id> --port 9999
  ghost act     --tool aws_ec2 --action RunInstances --params params.json --session-id <id>
  ghost evaporate --session-id <id>
  ghost replay  --session-id <id>
  ghost replay  --all --format csv
  ghost serve   --upstream http://localhost:8080 --port 7391

## Key Security Properties
- Ephemeral keys: private signing key exists only for the session TTL, then shredded
- Scope enforcement: agent can only call declared tools
- Tamper-evident residue: Ed25519-signed action chain, verifiable after evaporation
- Broker mode: real upstream credentials never reach the agent process
- Upstream key: always read from GHOST_UPSTREAM_KEY environment variable (never a CLI argument)

## Storage
Residue stored in SQLite (default: ~/.ghost/residue.db). Override with --db flag or GHOST_DB env var.

## Deployment (Docker)
Dockerfile available. Set GHOST_UPSTREAM_KEY as an environment variable in your container runtime — never pass it as a CLI argument.
  Port: 7391 (default)
  Env: GHOST_UPSTREAM_KEY, UPSTREAM_URL, PORT
